stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 5 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 8 observations with mean 75.3 points (within-school SD = 6.1). school B has 20 observations with mean 70.3 points (within-school SD = 7.0). school C has 5 observations with mean 78.3 points (within-school SD = 3.4). school D has 10 observations with mean 71.9 points (within-school SD = 8.0). school E has 8 observations with mean 62.1 points (within-school SD = 7.6). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 3.0 and 7.5 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school E, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school E: 64.1 points (shrunk from raw 62.1). Range [63.1, 66.0] as between-school SD varies from 3.0 to 7.5. Pooled mean: 70.9.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 70.9 points (weighted average of 5 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school E: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/8). At σ_between=5.0: B = 0.776.
Interval [63.1, 66.0] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (62.1) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (70.9) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
